mechanism_narrative: A persistently low federal funds rate near the zero lower bound increases the relative present value of future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of non-overlapping periods when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.5%, labor market deterioration signals weaker consumption and corporate revenue growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances when UNRATE exceeds 9.5%.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous month-over-month contraction in retail sales and industrial production indicates a broad demand-supply contraction that reduces earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and INDPRO fails to predict negative S&P 500 20-day returns with directional accuracy below 52% over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, accelerating industrial production growth signals a rebound in capacity utilization and operating leverage, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In quarters following GDP troughs, positive month-over-month INDPRO changes coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in retail sales signals stronger consumer spending momentum that translates into immediate earnings optimism, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month RSAFS increases coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, elevated inflation pressures prompt expectations of future monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year exceeds 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Non-farm payrolls expanding while the unemployment rate simultaneously declines indicates a robust labor market tightening that boosts consumption expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increase in PAYEMS and decrease in UNRATE fail to produce positive S&P 500 5-day returns with directional accuracy below 52% over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential investment and construction employment, which spills over into broader demand for durable goods and materials, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in HOUST coincide with negative S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations during the housing recovery phase.
